milestone: Transparent PNG uploads keep their alpha channel
slice: A real, user-reported bug in Builder V2: a transparent PNG dropped into a Scene rendered with its transparent regions replaced by solid black (the reported forest artwork showed a black cutout where the sky/gaps should have shown through). Traced to tools/world-builder-v2/js/worldBuilderApp.js's _downscaleImageDataURL (line ~6535), the AV-009 quota-safety re-encoder for uploads over 1.5MB: it unconditionally called canvas.toDataURL('image/jpeg', 0.85) regardless of the source's own format. JPEG has no alpha channel, so every transparent pixel got flattened to canvas-default black on the way through. Fixed by detecting a PNG source via its data URL's own MIME prefix ('data:image/png') and re-encoding as PNG (lossless, preserves alpha) in that case; JPEG sources still get JPEG encoding, so the compression path is unchanged for photographic uploads with no transparency to lose. The pre-existing 'out.length < dataURL.length ? out : dataURL' fallback still keeps the original bytes if a PNG re-encode happens to be larger than the source, so a photographic PNG that doesn't shrink under lossless re-encoding is left byte-identical to what was uploaded rather than blown up further. A disclosed trade-off, not a regression: preserving PNG can produce more bytes than a JPEG re-encode would, so a very large realistic-photograph PNG might now push closer to localStorage quota than before -- AV-009's own _writeAll/_persist() 'Save Failed -- try a smaller image' error already handles that case honestly, and correctness (transparency) wins over silent corruption. Verified via a real Playwright test against a 2400x2400 genuinely-transparent-with-a-hole PNG that actually exceeds the 1.5MB threshold: the transparent center pixel stays alpha=0 through the downscale (was flattening to solid black), the opaque region keeps its real color, and a real >1.5MB JPEG source is confirmed still to resolve to JPEG (no regression). The identical bug exists in tools/world-builder/ (v1) at line ~3571 and is disclosed but deliberately left untouched -- the user scoped this round to Builder V2 only. goldenBuild.js's full 30-assertion suite passes unchanged, confirming zero impact on the compile/validate/import pipeline.
